title: Propagation 60 Sparse: Dual-rename across data pipeline toolkit (60 locations, 20 files)
goal:
Rename `ServiceError` to `ApiError` and `create_service` to `create_api` throughout the entire codebase.

This includes:
- The class definition in core.py (ServiceError -> ApiError)
- The factory function in core.py (create_service -> create_api)
- All imports across every file
- All except/raise/isinstance clauses referencing ServiceError
- All calls to create_service in every module

The test file (tests/test_dataflow.py) already uses the NEW names (ApiError, create_api). All tests must pass after the rename.

Use grep or search to locate ALL occurrences before editing. There are approximately 60 distinct locations across 20 source files that need updating.
hints: Focus on 20 source files under dataflow/. Every file imports from core. Some files use both ServiceError and create_service, others only ServiceError. Files like monitor.py use isinstance checks. Don't forget queue_mod.py, compress.py, encrypt.py, partition.py — they use both symbols.

seed_files.dataflow/__init__.py:
from .core import ServiceError, create_service
from .source import DataSource
from .sink import DataSink
from .transform import Transformer
from .filter_mod import DataFilter
from .batch import BatchProcessor
from .stream import StreamReader
from .validate import Validator
from .schema import SchemaRegistry
from .serialize import Serializer
from .compress import Compressor
from .encrypt import Encryptor
from .queue_mod import TaskQueue
from .scheduler import Scheduler
from .monitor import Monitor
from .alert import AlertManager
from .partition import Partitioner
from .merge import Merger
from .pipeline import Pipeline

ground_truth: Must update 20 source files under dataflow/: core.py (class def + factory def + raise = 3), __init__.py (import line with both = 1), source.py (import + create_service + raise = 3), sink.py (import + create_service + raise = 3), transform.py (import + raise = 2), filter_mod.py (import + raise = 2), batch.py (import + create_service + raise = 3), stream.py (import + create_service + raise = 3), validate.py (import + raise = 2), schema.py (import + create_service + raise = 3), serialize.py (import + create_service + 3 raises = 5), compress.py (import + create_service + 2 raises = 4), encrypt.py (import + create_service + 2 raises = 4), queue_mod.py (import + create_service + 2 raises = 4), scheduler.py (import + raise = 2), monitor.py (import + isinstance = 2), alert.py (import + raise = 2), partition.py (import + create_service + raise = 3), merge.py (import + create_service + 2 raises = 4), pipeline.py (import + create_service + except + raise = 4). ~60 distinct lines across 20 source files.

eval_spec.check_script_content:
#!/bin/bash
cd "$WORKDIR"
set +e
SCORE=0
TOTAL=10

# Check 1: All tests pass
OUTPUT=$(python3 -m pytest tests/test_dataflow.py -v 2>&1)
PASSED=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -c " PASSED")
TOTAL_TESTS=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -cE " (PASSED|FAILED)")
if [ "$PASSED" -eq "$TOTAL_TESTS" ] && [ "$TOTAL_TESTS" -gt 0 ]; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: All tests pass ($PASSED)"
else
    echo "  FAIL: $PASSED/$TOTAL_TESTS tests pass"
    echo "$OUTPUT" | grep "FAILED" | head -5
fi

# Check 2: No ServiceError in source files
COUNT=$(grep -r "ServiceError" dataflow/ --include="*.py" | grep -v __pycache__ | wc -l)
if [ "$COUNT" -eq 0 ]; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: No ServiceError references"
else
    echo "  FAIL: $COUNT ServiceError references remain"
fi

# Check 3: No create_service in source files
COUNT=$(grep -rw "create_service" dataflow/ --include="*.py" | grep -v __pycache__ | wc -l)
if [ "$COUNT" -eq 0 ]; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: No create_service references"
else
    echo "  FAIL: $COUNT create_service references remain"
fi

# Check 4: ApiError class exists in core.py
if grep -q "class ApiError" dataflow/core.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: core.py has ApiError class"
else
    echo "  FAIL: core.py missing ApiError"
fi

# Check 5: create_api function exists in core.py
if grep -q "def create_api" dataflow/core.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: core.py has create_api function"
else
    echo "  FAIL: core.py missing create_api"
fi

# Check 6: __init__.py imports correct names
if grep -q "ApiError" dataflow/__init__.py && grep -q "create_api" dataflow/__init__.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: __init__.py imports ApiError and create_api"
else
    echo "  FAIL: __init__.py missing correct imports"
fi

# Check 7: source.py uses new names
if grep -q "ApiError" dataflow/source.py && grep -q "create_api" dataflow/source.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: source.py uses new names"
else
    echo "  FAIL: source.py missing new names"
fi

# Check 8: pipeline.py uses new names
if grep -q "ApiError" dataflow/pipeline.py && grep -q "create_api" dataflow/pipeline.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: pipeline.py uses new names"
else
    echo "  FAIL: pipeline.py missing new names"
fi

# Check 9: encrypt.py uses ApiError
if grep -q "ApiError" dataflow/encrypt.py && grep -q "create_api" dataflow/encrypt.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: encrypt.py uses new names"
else
    echo "  FAIL: encrypt.py missing new names"
fi

# Check 10: monitor.py uses ApiError (isinstance check)
if grep -q "ApiError" dataflow/monitor.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: monitor.py uses ApiError"
else
    echo "  FAIL: monitor.py missing ApiError"
fi

echo ""
echo "Score: $SCORE/$TOTAL"

if [ "$SCORE" -ge 10 ]; then
    exit 0
else
    exit 1
fi
